title: Der Freischן¿½tz
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Wie gehts, my frendts if youן¿½ll allow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I sings you rite afay shoost now
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Some dretful shdories vitch dey calls
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Freyschן¿½tz, or de Magic Balls.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Wohl in Bohemian land it cooms,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere folk trink prandy mate of plooms;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere lifed ein Yaeger Maxerl Schmit
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who shot mit goons und nefer hit.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Now dere vas von oldt Yaeger, who
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Says, ן¿½Maxerl, dis vill nefer do;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ If you shouldt miss on drial-tay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½ll pe der tyfel denn to bay.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½If you do miss, you shtupid coose,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dereן¿½ll pe de donnerwetter loose;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For you shant hafe mine taughterן¿½s hand,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Nor pe der Hertzhogן¿½s yaegersmann.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Id coomed pefore de tay vas set,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat all de shaps togeder met;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und Max he fired his goon und missed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und all de gals cot roundt und hissed.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey laughed pefore und hissed pehind;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot von shap-Kaspar saidt, ן¿½Tonן¿½t mind;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I dells you vot you stoons ן¿½em alls
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ If yoost you shoodt mit magic balls.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½De magic balls! oh, vot is dat?ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½I cot soom in my hoontinן¿½ hat;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Deyן¿½re plack as kohl, und shoodt so drue:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oh, demן¿½s de kindt of balls for you.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½You see dat eagle vlyinן¿½ high,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ein hoondred miles oop in de sky;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shoot at dat eagle mit your bix,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You kills hin tead ash doonderblix!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½I tonן¿½t pelieve de dings you say.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½You fool,ן¿½ says Kasp, ן¿½denn plaze afay!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He plazed afay, vhen, sure as plood,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Down coom de eagle in de mud.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½O was ist das?ן¿½ said Maxerl Schmit:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Vhy! datן¿½s de eagle vot you hit.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You kills him vhen you plaze afay;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot datן¿½s a ding you nix verstay.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Und you moost go to make dem balls
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To de Wolfן¿½s Glen vhen mitnight valls.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dow knowן¿½st de shpot alone und lateן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Oh ja I know shim ganz foost-rate!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Boot denn I does not like to co
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Among dem dings.ן¿½ Says Kasp, ן¿½Ach, ן¿½sho!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½ll help you fix dem tyfel chaps,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like a goot veller-dake some schnapps!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (ן¿½Hilf Zamiel! hilfן¿½) ן¿½Here, dake some more!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn Kasp vent shtompinן¿½ roundt de vloor,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und coomed his hoompugs ofer Schmit,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dill Max saidt, ן¿½Nun-ich gehe mit!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All in de finster mitternocht,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen oder folk in shleep vas lockt,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Down in de Wolfschlucht, Kasp tid dry
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His tyfel-strikes und Hexery.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit skools und pones he mate a ring,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De howls und shpooks pegin to sing,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und all the tyfels oonder croundt
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Coom preakinן¿½ loose und rooshinן¿½ roundt.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn Maxerl cooms along: says he,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Mein Gott! vot dings ish dis I see!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I dinks de fery tyfel und all
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Moost help to make dem magic ball.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½I vish dat I had nix cum raus,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und shtaid mineself in bett to house.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Hilf Zamiel!ן¿½ cried Kasp; ן¿½you whelp
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You red Dootch tyfel coom und help!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Den oop dere coomed a tredfull shdorm,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De todtengrips aroundt tid schvarm;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De howl shoomped oop und flopt his vings
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und toorned his het like avery dings.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oop droo de croundt dere coomed a pot
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit leadt, und dings to make de shot;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und h?llisch fire in grimson plaze,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und awful schmells like Schweitzer kase.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Agross de scene a pine-shtick flew
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit seferal shail-pirds vastened to;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Six treadtful shail-pirds mit deir vings
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Tied to de shticks mit magic shtrings.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All droo de air, all in a row,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Die wilde Jagd vas seen to go;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De hounds und teer all mate of pone,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und hoonted py a skilleton.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere coomed a tredful shpecdre pig,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Who, shpittenן¿½ fire afay, tid dig;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und fiery drocks und tyfel-shnake
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A scootinן¿½ droo de air tid preak.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot Kaspar tidnן¿½t mindt dem alls,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But casted out de pullet balls;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Six vas to go ash he vouldt like,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De seventן¿½ moost for de tyfel shtrike.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ad last, oopon de drial tay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De gals cot roundt so nice und gay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und den dey goed und maked a tantz,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und singed apout de Jungfernkranz.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und denn der Hertshog datן¿½s der Duke
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Cooms doun und dinks heן¿½ll dake a look;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Young mans,ן¿½ to Maxerl denn saidt he,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Shoost shoot dem dove oopon dat dree!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn Maxerl pointed mit de bix,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Potzblitz!ן¿½ says he, ן¿½dat dove Iן¿½ll fix!ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He fired his rifle at de Taubן¿½,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When Kass rollt ofer in de Staub.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De pride she falled too in de doost,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dey gals dey cried, de men dey got coossed:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Hertshog says, ן¿½Idן¿½s fery glear
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat dere has peen some tyfels here!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Und Max has shot mit tyfels-blei!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Pfui! die verfluchte Hexerei!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O Maximilian! O Du
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Gehst nit mit rechten Dingen zu!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Boot denn a hermits coomed in late;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Says he, ן¿½Iן¿½ll fix dese dings foostrate;ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und telled der Hertshog dat yung men
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vill raise der Tyfel now und denn.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De Duke forgifed de Kaspar dann,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und mate of him a Yegersmann,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhat shoodts mit bixen goon, und pfeil,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und talks apout de Waidmannsheil.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und denn de pride she coomed to life,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und cot to pe de Maxerlן¿½s vife;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Denn all de beoples gried ן¿½Hoorah!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Das ist recht brav! und hopsasa!ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ MORAL

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Py dis dings may pe oondershtood
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dat vhat is pad works ofden goot:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or, Maximilia maximilibus curantur if you will.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.
